commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=fc78d2db7e847ac41eac3f695ec0d9f05d881232)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a bundled Ask plugin with a Discord native `/ask` command, a new `plugin-sdk/discord-interactions` subpath, Discord native component reply registration, docs, tests, and workspace metadata.

Reproducibility: yes. for the main review findings: source inspection shows the proposed command is unscoped while current command contracts treat omitted channels as all-channel, and current Discord interaction dispatch pre-acknowledges before the handler. Live `/ask` behavior itself was not reproduced because the supplied proof is build/dist-only.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer product/API judgment and contributor-supplied real Discord proof; automation cannot supply the external PR proof gate or decide whether Ask belongs bundled in core.

target.title: feat: add minimal Discord /ask command
target.body:
## Summary
- add bundled ask plugin with a Discord native /ask command
- choose button/select/modal from the prompt and store log-only ask sessions/feedback
- expose a focused discord-interactions plugin-sdk subpath and register native command reply components so Discord callbacks reach plugin interactive handlers

## Safety
- /ask is answer-capture only: nextActionPolicy=log_only, requiresSecondGo=true, actionScope=answer_capture_only
- default allowedUsers is the invoking sender, components are non-reusable, sessions expire after 30 minutes
- no Poll routing, Select+Modal composite, approval execution, external send, config mutation, or long-running task execution in this PR

## Verification
- pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/ask/index.test.ts
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/native-command-reply.test.ts
- PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH pnpm tsgo:extensions
- PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH pnpm lint:extensions:bundled
- PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH pnpm check:architecture
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts
- node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-subpath-exports.mjs
- node scripts/sync-plugin-sdk-exports.mjs --check
- PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH pnpm build:strict-smoke

## Notes
- Local shell did not have a global pnpm binary, so a temporary shim to corepack pnpm was used for package scripts that shell out to pnpm.
- build:strict-smoke emitted existing zod locale CommonJS dts warnings from rolldown-plugin-dts, but completed successfully.
- A local rerun of check-extension-package-tsc-boundary.mjs --mode=compile stopped on diagnostics-otel resolving @opentelemetry/semantic-conventions from this workspace node_modules; the same CI job passed before this follow-up and the changed files do not touch diagnostics-otel.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:
  - Discord users can run native /ask and receive non-reusable interactive buttons/select/modal that record an answer only.
  - The built runtime includes the bundled ask plugin and the focused discord-interactions SDK export needed by the Discord interactive handler path.
- Real environment tested:
  - macOS local OpenClaw checkout built from this PR branch, Node.js via the repository toolchain, pnpm through corepack.
  - Built OpenClaw dist output, not a mocked unit-test fixture.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH pnpm build:strict-smoke
  - node dist/index.js --version
  - node script reading dist/extensions/ask/openclaw.plugin.json and checking generated dist files for ask and discord-interactions.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
$ PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH pnpm build:strict-smoke
CLI bootstrap import guard passed.
runtime-postbuild: plugin SDK root alias completed in 0ms
runtime-postbuild: bundled plugin metadata completed in 154ms
runtime-postbuild: official channel catalog completed in 3ms
runtime-postbuild: bundled plugin runtime overlay completed in 163ms
runtime-postbuild: static extension assets completed in 42ms
runtime-postbuild: stable root runtime imports completed in 112ms
runtime-postbuild: stable root runtime aliases completed in 86ms
runtime-postbuild: legacy root runtime compat aliases completed in 28ms
runtime-postbuild: legacy CLI exit compat chunks completed in 0ms
OK: All 4 required plugin-sdk exports verified.

$ node dist/index.js --version
OpenClaw 2026.5.19 (fc78d2d)

$ node - <<'NODE'
OK dist/extensions/ask/index.js
OK dist/extensions/ask/openclaw.plugin.json
OK dist/plugin-sdk/discord-interactions.js
OK dist/plugin-sdk/discord-interactions.d.ts
manifest id=ask name=Ask
export discord-interactions=true
NODE
```

- Observed result after fix:
  - The real built dist contains the ask bundled plugin manifest/runtime entry and the public discord-interactions SDK export.
  - The built CLI starts far enough to report the PR build stamp, confirming the generated runtime artifacts are loadable at the package level.
- What was not tested:
  - Live Discord /ask smoke against the running production gateway was not tested in this PR branch because the currently running global package is still the published runtime. That smoke should happen after merge/install/restart so the gateway is actually running this build.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):
  - Current production/global runtime does not include this PR build, so /ask is not available there yet.
target.author: ximanuki

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
